lead_profile_text: Rent-to-rent deal on 2-bed Sheffield property. Margins too thin after rent to landlord plus Stayful management fees plus setup costs. Geographic preferences not met — Sheffield not preferred area. Outcome: Abandoned — R2R financial model did not stack up.
emotional_profile: Entrepreneurial R2R investor. Numbers-focused. Abandoned quickly when financial model did not work.
lost_reason: R2R margin too thin — rent + Stayful management fees + setup costs leave insufficient profit; Sheffield not preferred geographic area

top_objections.text: R2R margins too thin after rent + management fees + setup costs
top_objections.zac_response: Financial analysis presented — deal structure does not stack up at proposed rent level

top_objections.text: Sheffield not the preferred geographic area for the lead
top_objections.zac_response: Geographic preferences discussed; Sheffield noted as suboptimal location for lead's operations

top_questions.text: Is this R2R deal financially viable with Stayful management?
top_questions.answer: No — margins after rent, management fees, and setup costs are insufficient

top_questions.text: What contract terms and management model would apply to an R2R arrangement?
top_questions.answer: Standard management model explained — but R2R adds rent layer that makes economics unworkable at this rent level

what_fell_flat: R2R financial model fundamentally unviable at proposed rent level
Sheffield geography not preferred by lead

conversion_gaps.gap: R2R model requires significant landlord rent reduction that rarely materialises
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Qualify R2R leads early on rent level vs STL gross revenue ceiling — if landlord asking price leaves thin margin before management fees and setup, advise walking away before investing meeting time
